key_insight: CEM-GMsFEM builds localized multiscale basis functions with spectral constraints and oversampling to accurately solve elliptic PDEs with highly heterogeneous, high-contrast coefficients.
engineering_relevance: It offers a robust surrogate finite element framework for multiscale solid mechanics and related PDE problems where fine-scale resolution is too expensive.
method: Construct spectral auxiliary spaces to capture dominant local modes.
Minimize energy under constraints to generate multiscale basis functions.
Use oversampling for localized computation and exponential decay of basis errors.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports rigorous error estimates and numerical simulations that confirm optimal convergence and exponential decay of the multiscale basis functions.
watch_item: Performance depends on a careful choice of auxiliary spaces, oversampling size, and local problem setup to preserve accuracy and efficiency.
paper_title: Multiscale modeling for problems with high contrast heterogeneous coefficients by the CEM-GMsFEM
